name: Pediatric Obesity (age-varying onset)
remarks: Custom module for the age-conditioned EHR study. Standard Synthea metabolic
syndrome gates obesity onset to age >= 18, which produces a FLAT pediatric
onset-age distribution. This module spreads obesity onset across developmental
bands (<1, 1-5, 6-11, 12-17, 18-25) with non-uniform, structured hazard so that
age carries real predictive signal for obesity onset.
Sets attribute 'peds_obese' = true at onset so the comorbidity modules
(T2D, OSA) can condition on it with age-varying latency.
Non-flat band probabilities. Realized onset is additionally censored by the
patient's attained age (a 3yo cannot realize an adolescent-onset track), so
the empirical onset-age distribution reflects both this structure and the
distribution_remarks: Never_Obese is intentionally the majority class so the negative pool
contains many LONG (older never-obese) timelines. This lets
build_disease_cohort's quantile length-matching realize the long
pre-onset contexts of older positives without exhausting the negative
pool -- neutralizing the age<->length confound (length-only AUROC ~0.5).
Early-weighted but still non-flat across all five bands. Onset is
concentrated in infancy/early/school age so a positive's PRE-ONSET
context length stays <= the never-obese negatives' full timelines
(which span the whole 0-25 range). This keeps build_disease_cohort's
age<->length confound while preserving age-varying onset structure.
